observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant:

Submit detailed schedule of the adjusting entry made on the account totaling ₱17,208,307.26.

Trace the above errors in the accounting records if these were included in the CIB year-end account balance, otherwise, prepare a JEV to reflect the same. 

Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the CIB account.

Always review the work of the accounting staff before finalization.

Prepare CY 2020 and prior years Bank Reconciliation Statements until the unidentified reconciling items from prior years are all identified.

Prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2022 and all months thereafter.
observations.reason:
Laxity of the officer concerned.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant:

Prepare and maintain General and Subsidiary Ledgers for all CIB-LCCA accounts in the SEF and TF.

Prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2022 and all months thereafter.
observations.reason:
Laxity of the officer concerned.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledgers for the Advances to Officers and Employees and ensure that Quarterly Report on Unliquidated Cash Advances is reconciled with accounting records.

Record grants of cash advance for travel of officers and employees as Advances to Officers and Employees instead of Due from Officers and Employees. Further, to make the necessary accounting entry to correct the error in the account used to record said cash advances.
observations.reason:
Laxity of the officer concerned and non-adherence to IPSAS and other accounting rules and regulations on the proper recognition of cash advances made by officers.

Verification of CY 2023 advances to officers for travelling expenses disclosed that the same were still recorded in the books as Due from Officers & Employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Local Chief Executive to constitute an Inventory Committee that will spearhead the conduct of physical count of Local Road Networks of the LGU. Likewise, to enjoin all officers concerned for the compliance of the guidelines set-out in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Municipal Accountant to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and pertinent records to support the balance of the account, compute and recognize depreciation, and reconcile the Report on Physical Count of Local Road Network with the accounting records. Further, to make a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as to the total road network system as at the end of the accounting period.

General Services Officer – Designate to maintain Local Road Network Property Cards, Local Road Inventory, and Road Map. Likewise, to prepare and submit at year-end a Report on the Local Road Network of the LGU.
observations.reason: Inaction of the officers concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the corresponding asset account for the infrastructure projects already completed at year-end. Likewise, to compute and recognize in the books the corresponding depreciation.
observations.reason:
Inaction of the officer concerned.

As of December 31, 2023, the CIP account has the same balance in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:

Stop recognizing as a subsidy all funds received from national government agencies, for these are fund transfer with specific purposes and requires the submission of Fund Utilization Report.

Maintain a General Journal to show any adjusting and/or correcting entries affecting the account. Likewise, maintain a General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledgers to support the balances presented in the Financial Statements.

Review the composition of the accounts Due to NGAs and Subsidy from NGAs, and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason:
Inaction of the officer concerned.

The review of the Fund Transfers for the CY 2023 audit, revealed that funds received with specific purpose were recorded as Subsidy.

Books were not submitted.

No submission of any correcting or adjusting entry to the account as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-

Make the required disclosure for the DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements in the forthcoming fiscal years.

We also recommended that Municipal Treasurer transfers to the Special Trust Fund bank account the unexpended DRRMF balance as determined by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer.
observations.reason:
Inaction of the officers concerned.

The required disclosure prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 was not complied with in the Notes to Financial Statements for the CY 2023 Financial Statements.

Moreover, actual transfer to the DRRMF Bank Account of unexpended balance pertains only to CY 2019, 2020 & 2021 as per Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepares a Monthly Certificate of Payment attached to the billing documents as a basis for the Municipal Budget Officer in approving requests for obligations and the Municipal Accountant for the computation of payments. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that every progress payment is subjected to the 10% retention money.
observations.reason:
Non-compliance with the required percentage to be withheld as retention money.

The post-audit of CY 2023 contract payments revealed that withheld retention money is 5% of the contract price. Moreover, the retention money was not regularly charged in each progress billing but rather a lump sum amount is withheld on the final billing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor all infrastructure projects to avoid delayed implementations; and,

Municipal Accountant to provide the computation of the liquidated damages on all delayed projects for deduction from the contractor’s claim and ensures the submission of the complete supporting documents for every progress billing.
observations.reason:
In-action of the officers concerned.

The same findings noted in the review of implementation of the projects under 20% Development Fund in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as the approving officer in the grants of cash advances must require the Municipal Accountant to have an updated monitoring of all cash advances and strictly implement the non-granting of additional cash advances to officers or employees with outstanding balances. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all officers and employees with outstanding balances as of December 31, 2022, copy furnished the Commission on Audit in order to facilitate the latter in taking appropriate action for that matter.
observations.reason: No demand was made to employees with cash advances of over 30 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine any errors in recording or posting to the above-mentioned accounts and immediately effect necessary correcting entries in order to present a fair and reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
In-action of the Municipal Accountant.

As of December 31, 2022, both the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds has a balance of 8,000.00 and 7,382,841.22, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer-Designate to strictly maintain the required records as provided in the NGAS for LGUs to facilitate review/examination of the Inventory account so as to ascertain reliability of the balances presented in the financial statements.

Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to conduct physical count of supplies and materials in order to aid the reconciliation with the Accounting Office’s records.
observations.reason:
Non-compliance of the officers concerned.

No Inventory Committee, hence, no physical count conducted.

As of December 31, 2023, the Inventory account has a balance of 1,431,213.00 which is not supported by a report on physical count of supplies and materials as at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the General Services Officer and all officers concerned to adhere to the guidelines on property and supply management laid out in the Handbook on Property and Supply Management and in the NGAS for LGUs in order to facilitate proper accounting recognition of inventories.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the criteria on the recognition of inventory and its subsequent recognition as expense in order to prevent any overstatement or understatement in the financial statement.
observations.reason:
Non-adherence on the proper accounting of inventories.

Still in practice is the outright recognition of expense upon purchase and making an adjustment at year-end. Such adjustment is without basis as there is no report on the physical count conducted at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the property records as mandated by existing rules and regulations in order that all items reported in the PPE account are properly supported with records.

Initiate the compliance of the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE account so that the PPE balance presented in the Financial Statements is verifiable as to completeness and existence.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify all depreciable assets and compute the related depreciation in order to prevent overstatement of the amount of PPE and Surplus presented in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Non-constitution of Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain that the fund transfers to which these liability accounts relate are already liquidated and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to have a fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Inaction of the officer concerned.

As of December 31, 2023, the said Other Payables account still has the same balance and is presented in the Statement of Financial Position as Deferred Credits/Unearned Income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze each transaction and adhere to the recognition criteria established for each account in order to fairly present the effects of these transactions on the financial statements. Likewise, to stop delegating the function of making a journal entry voucher to the staff or if it is inevitable, to ensure that the work of the staff is carefully reviewed. Furthermore, for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries.
observations.reason:
Inaction of the officer concerned.

Moreover, as per review of transactions for CY 2023, it was noted that there are still improper recognition of transaction in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose appropriately in the notes to financial statements any reconciliation resulting from the foregoing events.
observations.reason:
Non-compliance of the officer concerned.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the documentary requirements provided in the COA Circular and other regulations before signifying its approval for payment in the Disbursement Voucher in order that transactions are well supported to prove its validity and propriety and to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension if evidence warrants. 

Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the reglementary period on the submission of contracts directly to the COA Auditor to ensure timely review and compliance of any deficiencies that may be noted.
observations.reason:
Leniency of the officer concerned.

Contract payments made in CY 2023 are still lacking in supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.


Lack of support staff of the BAC Secretariat.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the Municipal Accountant on the preparation and submission of the required monthly and quarterly financial reports, schedules, and trial balances, including its supporting documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end financial results
observations.reason:
Laxity of the officer concerned.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Agency Head should supervise/monitor regularly the status of the submission of such reports, so that possible problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately
observations.reason: Laxity of officers concerned.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), for inspection, evaluation, and assessment to facilitate property insurance coverage consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that upon his attempt to apply for the existing GSIS Property Insurance policy, the said insurance does not cover typhoon, thus, he did not proceed with the application. However, the team required the Municipal Accountant to submit such proof to support the statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant adopt measures to address issues on the submission of the Financial Statements within the prescribed period. Further, to remind the Municipal Accountant that non-submission of Financial Statements warrants the suspension of salary in pursuance with Section 122 (a) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the accounts and reports to COA within the period prescribed in Section 347 of RA No. 7160, Section 7.2.1 paragraph (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006, Sections 361 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs, COA Circular No. 96-011 and relevant provisions of PD No. 1445.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of the audit of accounts and financial reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the General and Subsidiary Ledgers for all accounts and all funds, to support the balances in the Financial Statements and thus the accuracy and reliability of said financial statements could be ascertained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace the above-noted deficiencies with his accounting records as well as those contained in the prior years’ annual audit reports and make necessary adjusting entries when warranted;
Prepare bank reconciliation statements as required in Sec. 74 of PD 1445 and Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 for all of the bank accounts to support the balances reflected in the financial statements; and
Be mindful of the reglementary period within which to submit the said report to the intended recipients and its consequences if not complied with as laid down in Items 3.7, 3.8, and 3.9 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the proper recognition of transactions concerning the advance payment, progress billings, and retention money to present fairly the transactions in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall:
Trace the correct balance of the Due to NGA account and identify its source agencies to facilitate liquidation when warranted; and
Thereafter, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statement funds received from any government agencies composing the balance of the Trust Fund as of year-end in conformity with Item 27 of IPSAS 1 on Fair Presentation and Compliance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to existing standards on the recognition and presentation of financial transactions in the Financial Statements. Further, to provide complete information in the Notes to help users and stakeholders in the use and understanding of the amounts presented in the face of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with the budget records of the Municipal Budget Officer to accurately present the budget and actual amounts in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts.

We recommended that the:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate records or registries for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of balances and prevent any errors in the balance presented in the Statement of Comparison Budgeted and Actual Amounts concerning the said fund.
The Municipal Budget Officer to always record the gross amount for each progress billing without regard to any retention money withheld. Further, to initiate steps in order that the unrecorded cost for the Construction of Water System at Brgy. Sta. Cruz amounting to ₱149,714.17 be obligated under the available continuing appropriation for 20% Development Fund.
The officers concerned expressed their commitment to carry out the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineering Office to strictly monitor all infrastructure projects to ensure timely completion of the project and to prepare an Inspection Report and/or Statement of Work Accomplished as of the target completion date in order to correctly and accurately compute the cost of unperformed portion of the project as the basis for the liquidated damages due to the contractor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with the guidelines set forth in GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and the IRR of RA 9184, thus preventing the awarding of contract sans sufficient fund. Further, to request certification from the Municipal Budget Officer as to the availability of funds prior to the conduct of procurement.

We recommended that the management utilize the LDRRMF strictly for the implementation of projects and activities exclusively for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs:
The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that:
The signatories signed in the distribution list after the release of the stipend has been made including the dates when signed;
Returned any excess funds to the source agency, supported with official receipts before forwarding it to the Municipal Accountant for liquidation; and
Strictly comply with the reglementary period within which to issue a check for the refund of the unexpended balance to the source agency; and
The Municipal Accountant to double-check the supporting documents of the liquidation report and ensure that it has been properly recorded before submitting it to the source agency and the audit team in compliance with Section 4 Paragraphs 5 and 6 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall enjoin the responsible personnel in their faithful adherence to the policies, processes, and responsibilities outlined in Section 4(7) of PD No. 1445 and Items 4 & 5 of Local Budget Circular No. 144 dated February 28, 2022, and other pertinent laws, rules, and regulations in the implementation of government programs and projects in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned department heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned and effectively utilized to cater to the development needs of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer stop charging DRRM related expenditures to the prior years’ unexpended MOOE and QRF balances in the absence of an approved LDRRM Plan and AIP allowing such charges from said special trust fund.

We recommended that the:

BAC Secretariat immediately submit the lacking documents pertinent to the contract; and

Municipal Accountant requires documentary evidence to support the claim before signifying approval on the Disbursement Voucher. 

MDRRM Office as the implementing unit, submit the Course Outline/Agenda for the three-day activity, the name of participants or Attendance Sheets, and pictures.

We recommended that the officers concerned submit the mentioned lacking documentary requirements and that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat submits the contract and other pertinent procurement documents and be reminded of the reglementary period for the submission of the same to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

The Municipal Accountant to compute for the withholding tax on the OCA payments to the health care workers to which said tax is due and initiate the remittance of the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue;

The Health Care Workers concerned to refund the amount as computed by the Municipal Accountant representing withholding tax on the OCA they received; and

The RHU personnel concerned to submit to the accounting office all payrolls for the payment of allowances or other benefits in order for the latter’s review and compute any withholding tax that may be due therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Vice-Mayor and the Human Resources Management Officer:

Review the support staff functions requirement per office to determine the appropriate manpower complement of said office and exercise prudence in the hiring of JO workers; and

Ensure compliance with the hiring process and required documents by the HRMO, before JO employees officially report to work.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Immediately stop assigning JO personnel to the barangays which will perform work and duties redundant with those of the existing Barangay Officials and employees to avoid charges against the government fund wages for unnecessary services; and

Delegate the approval of JO’s DTR and accomplishments reports to officers/employees who perform the actual monitoring and supervision of their respective works to ascertain that a rendition of services had been made for the LGU before corresponding wages shall be paid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Vice-Mayor, to enjoin all officers and employees under their direct supervision to comply with the documentary requirements to substantiate any claim for traveling expenses.

Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to require a Liquidation Report in the format prescribed in Appendix 35 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, on all liquidations of cash advances for travel so as to ensure that documentary requirements are checked as complete before approving the same.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the regular and submission of BRS for all depository accounts maintained by the LGU.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower in the Accounting Office and delayed arrival of Bank Statements.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Agency Head should supervise/monitor the status of the submission of such reports, so that possible problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately
observations.reason: Incomplete attachments.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Accountant to comply immediately and religiously with the procedure and provisions of COA Circular 93-404 dated October 18, 1993 and file immediately or request for write-off of accountabilities in relation to the loss of documents and/or to be supported with the inventory of the vouchers, etc., duly validated by the Auditor. Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months. Adhere strictly with the provisions of the aforecited law and regulations
observations.reason: Management has no knowledge as to the request for relief or write-off of accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Accountant to comply immediately and religiously with the procedure and provisions of COA Circular 93-404 dated October 18, 1993 and file immediately or request for write-off of accountabilities in relation to the loss of documents and/or to be supported with the inventory of the vouchers, etc., duly validated by the Auditor. Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months. Adhere strictly with the provisions of the aforecited law and regulations
observations.reason: Management still has to consult with the Municipal Assessor for the necessary procedures to be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the land, land improvements and building be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with real property records of the municipal assessor
observations.reason: Management still has to consult with the Municipal Assessor for the necessary procedures to reconcile its recorded valuation.

observations.recommendation:
1.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to trace the above errors in the accounting records if these
were included in the CIB year-end account balance, otherwise, prepare a JEV to
reflect the same. And, maintain subsidiary ledgers for the CIB account. Likewise,
to initiate registration with Land Bank’s weAccess internet facility in order to
obtain bank statements.
b. The Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant copies
of the monthly snapshots.
We also recommended that the LCE provide the Accounting Department with at least
one (1) regular and capable staff to assist him in the preparation and maintenance of
the accounting records.

2.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to determine any errors in recording or posting to the above-mentioned accounts and
immediately effect necessary correcting entries in order to present a fair and reliable
balance in the financial statements.

3.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer-Designate to
strictly maintain the required records as provided in the NGAS for LGUs to
facilitate review/examination of the Inventory account so as to ascertain
reliability of the balances presented in the financial statements.
b. Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to conduct physical count of
supplies and materials in order to aid the reconciliation with the Accounting
Office’s records.

4.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the General Services Officer and all officers concerned to adhere to the
guidelines on property and supply management laid out in the Handbook on
Property and Supply Management and in the NGAS for LGUs in order to
facilitate proper accounting recognition of inventories.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the criteria on the recognition of
inventory and its subsequent recognition as expense in order to prevent any
overstatement or understatement in the financial statement.

5.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare
and maintain the property records as mandated by existing rules and regulations
in order that all items reported in the PPE account are properly supported with
records.
b. Initiate the compliance of the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular
No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE account so that the PPE
balance presented in the Financial Statements is verifiable as to completeness and
existence.
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to identify all depreciable assets
and compute the related depreciation in order to prevent overstatement of the
amount of PPE and Surplus presented in the Financial Statements.

6.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to carefully scrutinize each transaction in order to properly recognize the same in the
book of accounts and thus prevent any overstatement or understatement in the
financial statements. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary
correcting entries.

7.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to ascertain that the fund transfers to which these liability accounts relate are already
liquidated and make the necessary adjusting entries in order to have a fair presentation
in the financial statements.

9.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant
to carefully analyze each transaction and adhere to the recognition criteria established
for each account in order to fairly present the effects of these transactions on the
financial statements. Likewise, to stop delegating the function of making a journal
entry voucher to the staff or if it is inevitable, to ensure that the work of the staff is
carefully reviewed. Furthermore, for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary
correcting entries.

10.6. We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to
disclose appropriately in the notes to financial statements any reconciliation resulting
from the foregoing events.

11.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to execute a Memorandum of
Agreement for all funds transferred to implementing agency to ensure the latter's
compliance with the terms and conditions thereof and strictly impose its subsequent
liquidation.

12.13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the documentary requirements
provided in the COA Circular and other regulations before signifying its approval
for payment in the Disbursement Voucher in order that transactions are well
65
supported to prove its validity and propriety and to prevent the issuance of Notice
of Suspension if evidence warrants.
b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the reglementary
period on the submission of contracts directly to the COA Auditor to ensure timely
review and compliance of any deficiencies that may be noted.
c. Instruct the BAC or the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents.

13.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoins the Bids and Awards
Committee or the End-User to ensure that procurements are done in consonance to the
approved laws and regulations and that procedures are fully documented and the
necessary reports and documents are properly accomplished in order to facilitate
verification of the transaction’s validity and propriety. Furthermore, for the Local
Chief Executive to instruct the officers concerned on the above transactions to submit
the above-mentioned lacking documents.

14.11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to follow provisions of the COA
Circular 2012-002 in the preparation and submission of the Report on Utilization
of the LDRRMF;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to review the RAAO and submit corrected report to the
audit team after reconciliation with the accounting records;
c. The three officers to regularly reconcile their records on the LDRRMF to ensure
correctness of reports in the future.

15.13. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor current and succeeding
remittance of taxes withheld for purchases from supplier and coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer for the withholding of taxes and its timely remittance as
prescribed in Section 272 of the NIRC; and
b. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor his records of current and
succeeding financial assistance from any government agencies and strictly follow
the rules on liquidation as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the
stipulation in the MOA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers for transfer of unexpended disaster funds in order to substantiate the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its utilization.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents on fund utilization and liquidation in the future similar operation to ascertain that government resources are properly applied for and appropriately utilized.

We recommended that the Local Executive, being the Head of the LDRRMC, ensure that charges to the realigned LDRRMF are based on the identified allowable related expenses and those indicated in the covering local ordinance to maintain regularity and validity of transactions to serve the purpose of establishing the funds in future similar transactions.

We recommended that the Local Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for.
observations.reason: Physical inspection halted due to the pandemic. Officers concerned started the identification of these roads based on records of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant update its ledger cards to reflect the actual carrying value of the Biological Assets in order to achieve reliable and faithful presentation of Financial Statements. Also, enjoin the Municipal Agriculturist to initiate reconciliation of its records with the Municipal Accountant achieve the appropriate amount and accounts related to Biological Assets

We recommend that the Local Executive, being the Chairperson of the LDRRM Council, should strictly adhere to the proper selection of programs, projects, and activities in providing immediate disaster relief for the affected community.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant on the preparation of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMO on the regular submission or before the 15th day after the end of each month to COA Office in order to avoid delay in the delivery of audit results that supports significant management decisions

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to strategize and strengthen more its RPT collection drive by extending beyond the prescribed procedure of posting notices and take initiatives to reach directly the taxpayer’s attention to its respective obligations over realty taxes in order to realize its goals and objectives of providing more better services and facilities to its constituents

We recommended that Management shall prioritize the delivery of basic services and facilities for its constituents duly identified in the approved annual budget in adherence to the fundamental principles of local fiscal administration for the benefit of the greatest number of people
observations.reason: Delayed approval by the Local Sanggunian of the Annual Budget

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the Municipal Accountant on the preparation and submission of the required monthly and quarterly financial reports, schedules, and trial balances, including its supporting documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end financial results
observations.reason: Lack of manpower in the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: In order to achieve a self-reliant municipality aspired by the Local Government Code, we recommended that the Local Sangguniang Bayan shall initiate the amendment of certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or the promulgation of the Market Code that is effective and operative particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drive to boast revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management

We recommend that the Local Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory report and the related notes by asserting the lapsed transition policy on recognition of the same account, thus, affects the proper presentation and disclosure requirements necessary to achieve reliability for decision-making purposes
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 2013 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely of accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciation properties
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite by fencing of the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste for possible recycling. Also, in order to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies
observations.reason: The Dumpsite is now fenced. However, the allotted budget for the closure is insufficient. Management is waiting for the approval of the CY 2022 Annual Budget to fully implement the closure of the dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), for inspection, evaluation, and assessment to facilitate property insurance coverage consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.
observations.reason: No physical count of the insurable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers for transfer of
unexpended disaster funds in order to provide the required details in the Notes
to Financial Statements on the unexpended LDRRMF to substantiate the
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and provide confidence to the users of
Financial Statements over transparency and accountability of its utilization.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents on fund utilization and
liquidation in future similar operations to ascertain that government resources
are properly applied for and appropriately utilized.

We recommended that Management shall prioritize the delivery of basic services and facilities for its constituents duly identified in the approved annual budget in adherence to the fundamental principles of local fiscal administration for the benefit of the greatest number of people thereby defeating the primary purpose of its operations
observations.managementAction: IAR Convene the 2019 finance committee with the LCE and remind the strict adherence to the fundamental principles of local fiscal administration for the benefit of the greatest number of people

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply immediately and religiously with the provisions of COA Circular 93-404 dated October 18, 1993 and file request for write-off of accountabilities in relation to the loss of documents evidencing financial transactions and/or records of accountabilities supported with the inventory of the vouchers, etc., duly validated by the Auditor. Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months. Adhere strictly with the provisions of the aforecited law and regulations
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 -03 The Municipal Accountant implemented the request for write-off but vouchers are nowhere to be found after the super typhoon Yolanda struck and the municipality requested for write-off of accountabilities in relation to the loss of documents evidencing financial transactions and/or records of accountabilities supported with the inventory of the vouchers, etc., duly validated by the Auditor. Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged and lost properties and equipment. Instruct the GSO to comply religiously with the documentation requirement and the process in the filing of request for relief from property accountability. As soon as the relief is granted, instruct the Accountant to draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry to drop from the books the lost items

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all existing depository accounts within the prescribed timeframe and adjust the control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly. Direct all concerned officials to perform faithfully their duties pursuant to Section 444 (b.1 (x)) of RA 7160
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 -02 The Accounting Office is not regularly furnished with the monthly snapshot or statement

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the land, land improvements and buildings be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with real property records of the municipal assessor

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive strongly enjoin the Municipal
Accountant on the strict compliance with the required presentation and disclosure of the
road network in the financial statements to achieve reliability for decision-making
purposes

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
update its ledger cards to reflect the actual carrying value of the Biological Assets in
order to achieve reliable and faithful presentation of Financial Statements. Also,
enjoin the Municipal Agriculturist to initiate reconciliation of its records with the
Municipal Accountant to achieve the appropriate amount and accounts related to
Biological Assets.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall confer with the Municipal
Budget Officer on the strict adherence to the established guidelines on appropriations
and utilization of 20% DF

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, being the Chairperson of the
LDRRM Council, should strictly adhere to the proper selection of programs, projects,
and activities in providing immediate disaster relief for the affected community

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant on the
preparation of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and enjoin
the designated LDRRMO on the regular submission thereof on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month to COA Office in order to avoid delay in the delivery of audit
results that supports significant management decisions

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive order the Municipal Agriculturist to
earnestly demand from the Supplier on the compatible accessory to achieve
functionality of the acquired agricultural machinery, otherwise, legal assistance from
the Provincial Legal Officer must be sought against the defaulting Supplier’s failure
to complete its delivery, to eventually realize the program’s objective of economic and
social condition restoration to higher level resiliency

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to
strategize and strengthen more its RPT collection drive by extending beyond the
prescribed procedure of posting notices and take initiatives to reach directly the
taxpayer’s attention to its respective obligations over realty taxes in order to realize its
goals and objectives of providing more better services and facilities back to its
constituents

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor on the PS limitation with the correct limitation as prescribed in the local code to maintain balance and not to exceed on the succeeding budget year
observations.managementAction: IAR 2019 -02 The MBO committed on strict monitoring not to exceed correct limitation as prescribed in the local code to maintain balance and not on the succeeding budget year

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Planning and Development Officer in collaboration with the Budget Officer to make representations with the Local Sanggunian on the realignment of the items included in the recapitulation of the priority projects and direct them for the attainment of economic development and environmental management that redounds to the benefit of the constituents
observations.managementAction: IAR 2019 -03 The Municipal Accountant justified that items identified not chargeable against the EDF were not contracted during the calendar year but realigned to other infrastructure projects

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall adhere to the disclosure requirements in the Notes to Financial Statements to enhance the understanding of the readers and users of the financial results

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall use the “Other General Services” account under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses to record the wages of JOs except those that can be specifically identified related to the prosecution of civil works that an appropriate “Repairs and Maintenance” account will be used. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer shall maintain its registry and update the Local Chief Executive on the appropriation absorption capacity to accommodate additional temporary personnel so as not to exceed the limits established by law. And also, the Local Chief Executive shall be cautious with the limits provided by law to achieve balanced budget and fair presentation of financial results
We recommended henceforth, prepare and include the statement financial performance for trust fund in the consolidated financial statement

We recommended that the Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the Municipal Offices to the preparation and submission of the required monthly and quarterly submission of financial reports, schedules and trial balances, including its supporting documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end financial results
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 -04 The Mayor shall monitor the compliance of the Municipal Offices to the preparation and submission of the required monthly and quarterly submission of financial reports, schedules and trial balances, including its supporting documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end financial results

observations.recommendation: In order to achieve a self-reliant municipality aspired by the Local Government Code, we recommended that the Local Sangguniang Bayan shall initiate the amendment of certain provisions in the Tax Ordinance or the promulgation of the Market Code that is effective and operative particularly on penalty provisions in case of defaults. And shall direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and mobilize for revenue generation drive to boast revenue collections including improvement of economic enterprise operation and management
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 -05 Needs to confer with the Sanggunian

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ascertain that hiring of Job Orders be covered by the required contract of service and capacitate the designated HRMO for the screening and staffing to ascertain the capabilities and skills of hired personnel for the job at hand

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive emphasize the functions of the designated Human Resource Management Officer particularly on manpower planning, screening, selection, staffing needs in order to achieve balanced and equally distributed tasks to job order personnel

We recommend that Management create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 -01; 2016 -01; 2015 -01; 2013 -02 The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry to book-up the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF balance in the General Fund to a Trust Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRMF” in order to establish the same for the sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 -02 Separate bank account was established for the purpose supporting the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF balance in the General Fund to a Trust Special Trust Fund for the sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the 1% allocation from the IRA to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter for the welfare of the nation’s future human capital assets

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of loan repayments by imposing the actual amount billed and consequently remit the same promptly to the GSIS. The active practice of partial or non-deduction of payroll shall strongly be discontinued in adherence to the requirements of the GSIS Act that redounds on the benefits accorded to the active and separated personnel of the government

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee shall ensure that only related PPAs be considered in the GAD Plan and Budget during deliberations with due considerations on the guidelines and prohibitions in the allocation and attribution thereof so as to serve the purpose of establishing the said GAD Fund and not redirect government financial resources from other priority projects of the municipality

We recommended the concerned officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 -02 The LCE should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials: a. To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the specific nature of work or duties and responsibilities and the desired work outputs of the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and evaluation of the accomplishments of these personnel. b. To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Agency Head should supervise/monitor regularly the status of the submission of such reports, so that problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 -02 The Agency Head should supervise/monitor regularly the status of the submission of such reports, so that problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) for inspection, evaluation, and assessment to facilitate property insurance coverage consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 -06 Allocation for insurance has been made but process of enrollment for property insurance was delayed due to the current crisis

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry to book-up the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF into the Trust Fund using ‘Trust Liability-DRRMF’ account in the Trust Fund thereby establishing the same for the sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the GSO and Accountant to prepare their respective property records.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Accountant to comply immediately and religiously with the provisions of COA Circular 93-404 dated October 18, 1993 and file request for write-off of accountabilities in relation to the loss of vouchers, etc., duly validated by the Auditor. Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months. Adhere strictly with the provisions of the aforecited law and regulations.
observations.reason: Management explained that these vouchers and other documents were ruined by the typhoon.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged and lost properties and equipment. The GSO should comply religiously with the documentation and process in the filing of request for relief from property accountability. As soon as the relief is granted, instruct the Accountant to draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry to drop from the books the lost items.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the GSO and Accountant to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged and lost properties and equipment.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the GSIS for inspection, evaluation, and assessment to facilitate the application for property insurance coverage with the GSIS of all insurable properties.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of LCE to require concerned officials to facilitate the inspection, application for property insurance assessment to with the GSIS of all insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all existing depository accounts within the prescribed timeframe and adjust the control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly. Direct all concerned officials to perform faithfully their duties pursuant to Section 444 (b.1 (x)) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Administrative sanctions are not imposed to the Accountant on his failure to comply with his responsibilities.

requirements in the Notes to Financial Statement to enhance the understanding of
the readers and users of the financial results

Services” account under Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses classification of
expenses to record the wages of JOs except those that can be specifically
identified related to the prosecution of civil works that an appropriate “Repairs and

absorption capacity to accommodate additional temporary personnel so as not to
exceed the limits established by law. And also, the Local Chief Executive shall be
cautious with the limits provided by law to achieve balanced budget and fair

include the statement financial performance for trust fund in the consolidated
financial statement to achieve fair presentation thereof

Municipal Accountant on the preparation and submission of the required monthly
and quarterly financial reports, schedules and trial balances, including its supporting
documents, in order to afford the LGU of the earlier submission of the year-end

planning, screening, selection, and staffing needs in order to achieve balanced and
equally distributed tasks to job order personnel

Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
