observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the GSO and Accountant to prepare property their respective property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive ensure the 1% allocation from the IRA to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter

We recommend the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office to persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding tranches, and enjoin the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the implementation and completion of BuB projects

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of loan repayments by imposing the actual amount billed and consequently remit the same promptly to the GSIS. The practice of partial or non-deduction of loan amortizations from the respective payroll shall strongly be discontinued in adherence to the requirements of the GSIS Act that redounds on the benefits accorded to the active and separated personnel of the government

We recommend that Local Finance Committee ensure that only related PPAs be considered in the GAD Plan and Budget during budget deliberations with due considerations on the guidelines and prohibitions in the attribution thereof so as to serve the purpose of establishing the said GAD Fund and not redirect government financial resources from other priority projects of the municipality

We recommend the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same.
observations.reason: Management still have not submitted/provide the required information

observations.recommendation: We recommend the LCE: a. To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the specific nature of the work or duties and responsibilities and the desired work outputs of the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and evaluation of the accomplishments of these personnel. b. To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.reason: No submission of the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE: a. Discuss with contractors the error in the preparation of the ABC and require them to refund the excess mark up on cost of projects for the completed projects; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to deduct the claims of contractors for the excess VAT for on-going and unpaid projects; and c. Require the Municipal Engineer’s Office to adhere to DPWH Order No. 22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the ABC in all of its projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the MPDO to conduct close monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation of barangay projects, especially those that the municipality has subsidized. We also recommend management prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) prior to the release of funds to the barangays containing therein the terms, conditions and obligations of the source and implementing agencies, particularly the submission of accomplishment or liquidation reports.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to plan, focus and undertake definite activities which will: a. Enhance women’s access to all types of health care; b. Lower maternal mortality rates and provide adequate family planning services; c. Enhance the situation of women in the informal economy; d. Encourage women victims of violence to report their ordeal.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials to comply with the regulations provided in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01 on the utilization of the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LCE should require the LDRRM to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.

It is recommended that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.
observations.reason: Still looking for Land to be acquired for sanitary landfill. Open dumpsite has already been closed.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to be given to accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that Management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.

Require the Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Agency Head should supervise/monitor regularly the status of the submission of such reports, so that possible problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately.
observations.reason: Monthly reports are submitted beyond due date. The Agency should supervise/monitor the status of submission.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Mayor to submit all the required documents for the cash advances for intelligence fund, and to comply with the corresponding liquidation procedures, and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Transmittal Letter to the Office of the Chairman or a copy of the Credit Notice from the COA Chairman. Henceforth, management should comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 to avoid recurrence of this observation and to facilitate the immediate audit of the intelligence funds for prompt communication of audit results.
observations.reason: Cash advances of Municipal Officials and employees were Refunded through salary deductions.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the BAC Chairman, the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Accountant to submit approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) days from perfection thereof. The Municipal Mayor should always supervise and monitor the on time submission of these vital documents to the Audit Team, for a timely review/audit.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) whenever properties are issued to officials or employees.
Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply with the cited provisions of the law and regulations.
It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the land, land improvements and buildings be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with real property records of the municipal assessor.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to require concerned officials to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall recognize the required
depreciation and possible impairment of PPE accounts to reflect its proper value and
measurement in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare a Journal Entry, as illustrated below, to book-up the transfer of the
unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF balance in the General Fund to a Special Trust
Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRMF” in order to establish the same for the
sole purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the
next five (5) years:

LGU Books
Particulars General Fund Trust Fund
Transfer of unexpended Quick (Dr.) Subsidy to Other Funds (Dr)Cash
Response and Mitigation Funds- (Cr). Cash (Cr)Trust Liability- DRRMF
MOOE (with subsidiary for the source
and year)

We recommend the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the 1% allocation from the IRA
to strengthen its Local Councils for the Protection of Children and to support the
implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the latter for the welfare of
the nation’s future capital assets

We recommend the Local Chief Executive shall require the Accounting Office to
persistently follow-up the immediate release of the succeeding fund tranches, and enjoin
the Engineering Office to strictly observe the respective timetables in the
implementation and completion of BuB projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to
intensify the collection of loan repayments by imposing the actual amount billed and
consequently remit the same promptly to the GSIS. The practice of partial or non-
deduction of loan amortizations from the respective payroll shall strongly be
discontinued in adherence to the requirements of the GSIS Act that redounds on the
benefits accorded to the active and separated personnel of the government.

We recommend that the Local Finance Committee shall ensure that only GAD related
PPAs be considered in the GAD Plan and Budget during budget deliberations with due
considerations on the guidelines and prohibitions in the allocation and attribution
thereof so as to serve the purpose of establishing the said GAD Fund and not redirect
government financial resources from other priority projects of the municipality

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all their respective properties of the LGU. Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could be completely or accurately accounted for. The LCE should compel the GSO and the Accountant to prepare property records. Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold good local governance through transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials: a. To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the specific nature of the work or duties and responsibilities and the desired work outputs of the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and evaluation of the accomplishments of these personnel. b. To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the LCE: a. Discuss with contractors the error in the preparation of the ABC and require them to refund the excess mark up on cost of projects for the completed projects; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to deduct the claims of contractors for the excess VAT for on-going and unpaid projects; and c. Require the Municipal Engineer’s Office to adhere to DPWH Order No. 22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the ABC in all of its projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the MPDO to conduct close monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation of barangay projects, especially those that the municipality has subsidized. We also recommend that management prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) prior to the release of funds to the barangays containing therein the terms, conditions and obligations of the source and implementing agencies, particularly the submission of accomplishment or liquidation reports.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to plan, focus and undertake definite activities which will: a. Enhance women’s access to all types of health care; b. Lower maternal mortality rates and provide adequate family planning services; c. Enhance the situation of women in the informal economy; and d. Encourage women victims of violence to report their ordeal.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials to comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.

We recommend that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also, Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.

Require all accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to the same accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the requirement of public bidding on all procurement as mandated in Section 10 of Republic Act 9184. Alternative method of procurement is justifiable provided it complies with the conditions on Alternative Method as provided in Section 54 of the same law.

We recommend that management require the BAC and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and its IRR, particularly, Sections 10 and 54.1 and Item 2(a), Annex “H” of the IRR.

We recommend management observe the rules and regulations on the deductions from the salaries of employees as provided in the General Appropriations Act issued every year before entering into Memorandum of Agreement to avail loans for its employees from private lending institutions. Let the individual employees remit their loan payments to these lending institutions.

We recommend that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the required documents to validate the authenticity of the procured items. In addition, the Report on Drugs and Medicines Issued for all issued drugs and medicines should be prepared and submitted to the Accountant to serve as basis in recording the used/ expense portion in the books. Henceforth, pay only the transactions with complete supporting documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the disbursement for ESA and CBLA. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly COA Circular No. 94-013 on the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents for fund transfers received from other government agencies.

We recommend that Management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG-MC dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.
We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Economic Development Fund.

Require the Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Agency Head should supervise/monitor regularly the status of the submission of such reports, so that problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately.

Require the Municipal Mayor to submit all the required documents for the cash advances and to comply with the corresponding liquidation procedures, and to furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Transmittal Letter to the Office of the Chairman or a copy of the Credit Notice from the COA Chairman. Henceforth, management should comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 2003-003 to avoid recurrence of this observation and to facilitate the immediate audit of the intelligence funds for prompt communication of audit results.

Instruct the BAC Chairman, the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Mayor to submit approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) days from perfection thereof. The Municipal Mayor should always supervise and monitor the on time submission of these documents to the Audit Team, for a timely review/audit.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) whenever properties are issued to officials or employees.
Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the cited provisions of the law and regulations.
Instruct the Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for the specified months. Adhere strictly with the provisions of the aforecited law and regulations.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged and lost properties and equipment. Instruct the GSO to comply religiously with the documentation requirement and the process in the filing of request for relief from property accountability. As soon as the relief is granted, instruct the Accountant to draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry to drop from the books the lost items.

Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) for inspection, application for evaluation, and property insurance assessment consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

Require the Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all existing depository accounts and adjust the control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly. Direct all concerned officials to perform faithfully their duties pursuant to Section 444 (b.1 (x)) of RA 7160.
It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the land, land improvements and buildings be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with real property records of the municipal assessor.

We recommend that management follow strictly with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 7, 2012 on the use of Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF). The LDRRM Council have to ensure that lined programs and activities under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention of the LCE to require concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to submit all financial reports with complete supporting documents within the reglementary period. The Disbursement Head should regularly supervise/monitor the status of the submission of such reports, so that problems/causes of delays could be solved immediately.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to compel the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Agency Accountant to submit accounts as required by regulations.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the BAC Chairman, the Municipal Engineer, and the Municipal Accountant to submit approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within five (5) working days after its execution. The Municipal Mayor should always supervise and monitor the on submission of these vital documents to the Audit Team, for a timely review/audit.
observations.reason: The LCE cannot compel the BAC to submit contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LGU officials concerned to submit immediately the above listed reports with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strictly comply the cited provisions of the law and regulations.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to require concerned officials to comply with the cited provisions and to submit required LDRRMF reports.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged and lost properties and equipment. Instruct the GSO to comply with the documentation and process in the filing of request for relief from property accountability. As soon as the relief is granted, instruct the Accountant to draw the appropriate adjusting journal entry to drop from the books the lost items.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to direct the accountant and GSO to speed up the verification process and preparation of the list and book value of all damaged properties and equipment.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all existing depository accounts within the prescribed timeframe and adjust control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly. Direct all concerned officials to perform faithfully their duties pursuant to Section 444 (b.1 (x)) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Administrative sanctions are not imposed to the Accountant on his failure to comply with his responsibilities. The Accountant has not diligently performed bank reconciliations.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the land, land improvements and buildings be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with real property records of the municipal assessor.
observations.reason: Lack of intervention on the part of the LCE to require concerned officials to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that
will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU.
Their reports will be reconciled with the book balances, and the
differences should be properly accounted for. The periodic physical inventory shall
strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102
and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible
for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU could
be completely or accurately accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be
properly accounted, disposed and recorded.
Also, we recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare
and update a schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date,
useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net
book balance of each item of depreciable properties.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials
to comply with the regulations provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to uphold
good local governance through transparency and accountability. Also, the LCE
should require the Municipal Engineer to inform our Office within ten (10) days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the
program/project/activity that the appropriate projects signboards and/or public
notices are already posted at the project site in order for us to validate the same.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials:
a. To stipulate in the Job Order Contract or Contract of Service the
specific nature of work or duties and responsibilities and the desired
work outputs of the JO personnel to facilitate comparison and
evaluation of the accomplishments of these personnel.
b. To submit copies of Job Order Contract or Contract of Service to the
Auditor within five (5) days from its issuance as required in COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that the LCE:
a. Discuss with contractors the error in the preparation of the ABC and
require them to refund the excess mark up on cost of projects for the
completed projects;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to deduct the claims of contractors
for the excess VAT for on-going and unpaid projects; and
c. Require the Municipal Engineer’s Office to adhere to DPWH Order No.
22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the ABC in all of its projects.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the MPDO to conduct
close monitoring and periodic evaluation/inspection on the status of implementation
of barangay projects, especially those that the municipality has subsidized. We also
recommend that management prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) prior
to the release of funds to the barangays containing therein the terms, conditions and
obligations of the source and implementing agencies, particularly the submission of
accomplishment or liquidation reports.

We recommend that the LCE require the concerned officials to comply with the
regulations provided in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-01
on the utilization of the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF).
The LCE should require the LDRRM to ensure that lined programs and activities
under the 70% LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during
the year.

We recommend that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of
the current open dump site by fencing the perimeter area and assigning personnel to
segregate biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes for possible recycling. Also,
Management must provide an adequate appropriation to fully implement its Solid
Waste Management Plan and ensure effectiveness and sustainability of its Solid
Waste Management Program. Barangays should be required to create Barangay
SWM Committee and prepare and implement solid waste management policies.

The Local Chief Executive should create and Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. Then, the Property Custodian shall prepare and the periodic physical inventory shall strictly be conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Sections 102 and 111 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 80-124. The GSO should be responsible for the submission of the required RPCPPE, so that all properties of the LGU will completely/accurately be accounted for, and the unserviceable properties be properly accounted, disposed and recorded. Instruct the Accounting Department to prepare and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Cards and to prepare and update the schedule of depreciation expense showing the cost, acquisition date, useful life, monthly depreciation charges, accumulated depreciation and net book balance of each item of depreciable properties.
observations.reason: Inventory of properties still is not completed and the LCE cannot compel the GSO and the Accountant to prepare property records.

observations.recommendation: Require all concerned accountable officers to immediately liquidate all cash advances and that current cash advances for travel should first be settled before new ones be allowed to be granted in compliance with the cited rules in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the requirement of public bidding on all procurement as mandated in Section 10 of Republic Act No. 9184. Alternative method of procurement is justifiable provided it complies with the conditions on Alternative Method as provided in Section 54 of the same law.

We recommend that management require the BAC and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and it’s IRR, particularly, Sections 10 and 54, and Item 2(a), Annex “H” of the IRR.

We recommend that management observe the rules and regulations on the authorized deductions from the salaries of employees as provided in the General Appropriations Act issued every year before entering into Memorandum of Agreement to avail loans for its employees from private lending institutions. Let the individual employees remit their loan payments to these lending institutions.
observations.reason: There were still remittances to private lending institutions during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant be required to submit immediately the required documents to validate the authenticity of the procured items. In addition, the Report on Drugs and Medicines Issued for all issued drugs and medicines should be prepared and submitted to the Accountant to serve as basis in recording the used/ expense portion in the books. Henceforth, pay only the transactions with complete supporting documents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit immediately the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the disbursement for ESA and CBLA. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly COA Circular No. 94-013 on the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents for fund transfers received from other government agencies.
We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 on the use of 20% Economic Development Fund.

Require the Municipal Mayor to submit all the required documents for the cash advances for intelligence fund, to comply with corresponding procedures, and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Transmittal Letter to the Office of the Chairman or a copy of the Credit Notice from the COA Chairman. Henceforth, management should comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 2003-003 to avoid recurrence of this observation and to facilitate the immediate audit of the intelligence funds for prompt communication of audit results.
observations.reason: Lack of intent on the part of the LCE to comply with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to accomplish the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (ARE) whenever properties are issued to officials or employees.
observations.reason: There were still some properties issued to employees that do not have the corresponding ARE.

observations.recommendation: Submit immediately the list of insurable properties to the GSIS for inspection, evaluation, and assessment to facilitate property insurance coverage consistent with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR, as well as the guidelines per Manual of Procedures on Goods and Services. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension/Disallowance for all adverse findings discovered in the post-audit of the transaction documents.
We recommended that the Revenue Collection Clerks be required to turn over their collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-383 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the drivers concerned be required to submit duly approved and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets and to render Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. Henceforth management should strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975 and Section 4(2) of PD 1445.
